Defendant City And County Of San Francisco?S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Declare Plaintiff A Vexatious Litigant And Request For Prefiling Order And Order Requiring Plaintiff To Furnish Security
Off Calendar Oct-03-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Patrick Kelly, Esq., of City Attorney, Tel:(415) 554-3856 for defendant CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO.
Court Reporter: Tamara Willat-Harrison CSR# 4609 email: tamarawillat@sbcglobal.net Tel: 415.272.1753
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing held. A timely Notice of Contest was received from Plaintiff; however, Plaintiff did not appear at the hearing. The matter was discussed on the record. Having reviewed the pleadings, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

2 - DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO Defendant City And County Of San Francisco?S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Declare Plaintiff A Vexatious Litigant And Request For Prefiling Order And Order Requiring Plaintiff To Furnish Security

Defendant's motion to declare plaintiff Agardi a vexatious litigant is GRANTED.

Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted.

Code of Civil Procedure section 391(b) defines a "vexatious litigant," among other things, as:

(2) After litigation has been finally determined against the person, repeatedly relitigates or attempts to relitigate, in propria persona, either (i) the validity of the determination against the same defendant or defendants as to whom the litigation was finally determined or (ii) the cause of action, claim, controversy, or any of the issues of fact or law, determined or concluded by the final determination against the same defendant or defendants as to whom the litigation was finally determined.

(3) In any litigation while acting in propria persona, repeatedly files unmeritorious motions, pleadings or other papers, conducts unnecessary discovery, or engages in other tactics that are frivolous or solely intended to cause unnecessary delay.

Defendant has established Plaintiff meets both criteria. Plaintiff has been litigating the library's hacked computers and alleged stalker since 2007. (RJN Ex. 6 at pars. 2, 4, 24, 36-38.) The Superior Court found the allegations uncertain and sustained defendant's demurrer. (RJN Ex. 7.) The Court of Appeal affirmed and described her allegations as concerning "Agardi's belief that some unknown person was hacking into her computer files through City library computers." (RJN Ex.9 at p. 2.) Plaintiff now seeks to re-litigate the hacking issues. In addition, the court stayed discovery in this case. Despite that order, plaintiff continues to attempt to conduct discovery. (Kelly Decl., Exs. A-O.) Plaintiff's conduct and flouting of the court's stay order is frivolous.

Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 391.7(a), the court issues a pre-filing order. Plaintiff must obtain leave from the presiding judge of the court before filing any new action. Violation of this order is punishable by contempt.

Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 391.1, Plaintiff shall post a security of $45,000 by October 24, 2025, or risk dismissal of the action. (See Kelly Decl. par. 8.)

(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Order is signed.

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-09-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
S. PATRICK KELLY, Esq., Deputy City Attorney (415) 554-3856 patrick.kelly@sfcityatty.org Attorney for Defendant CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
No other appearances.

Hearing held. Discussed, not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's demurrer to all causes of action in plaintiff Juliana Agardi's third amended complaint (TAC) is sustained with one final opportunity provided to Ms. Agardi to allege, if she can do so in good faith, one or more intelligible viable claims. If Ms. Agardi chooses to file a fourth amended complaint, she must do so no later than July 30, 2025. The discovery stay in the May 2, 2025 order remains in effect.

In her 69 page TAC Ms. Agardi purports to allege five causes of action (although more than that are listed on the first page), all arising out of a three month suspension of her library privileges, seeking $20 million dollars. The TAC, and all purported causes of action therein, are, like her two prior complaints, uncertain and unintelligible. Ms. Agardi has ignored the guidance in the January 16, 2025 order that she should "provide a short summary of the pertinent facts for each cause of action" and instead includes numerous documents, the relevance of which is unclear. Most significantly, Ms. Agardi has failed to heed the guidance provided by the May 2, 2025 order.

While the court recognizes that Ms. Agardi is representing herself and may not have any legal training, she is not excused from the basic rule that, for each cause of action, she must succinctly allege ultimate facts supporting each element of that cause of action. Because she is suing a governmental entity for alleged violation of California law, Ms. Agardi must also allege compliance with the Government Code claims requirements or a legally justifiable excuse for not doing so. Ms. Agardi should also refrain from including irrelevant matters and should keep her inclusion of other documents to the minimum needed to support any of her claims.

Order signed.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk; W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/HEK)
